column1: # mapping_set_title: Open Policy Agent (OPA) to MITRE D3FEND Mappings
# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between Open Policy Agent (OPA)
#   LinkML classes (Rego policy language, bundle manifest, authorization input,
#   capabilities, intermediate representation plan) and MITRE D3FEND defensive
#   technique classes. D3FEND is the highest-confidence interoperability target
#   for OPA because D3FEND already models the exact authorization / access
#   control concepts that OPA implements at runtime (AuthorizationService,
#   AccessPolicyAdministration). Predicates - skos:closeMatch where the OPA
#   class and D3FEND technique class describe the same operational artefact
#   (e.g. a policy decision input or an access-control configuration document);
#   skos:relatedMatch where the OPA class is one ingredient or implementation
#   path of a broader D3FEND technique (e.g. a Rego built-in administering
#   policy); skos:broadMatch where the OPA class is a delivery container that
#   subsumes multiple D3FEND artefacts (e.g. a bundle Manifest carrying many

column10: OPA AuthorizationInput is the canonical input document evaluated by an authorization decision; semantically equivalent to D3FEND Authorization as a defensive artefact representing a single authorization act
An AuthorizationInput is consumed by an authorization service; related but not identical (one is the request payload, the other is the service)
An AuthorizationInput frequently carries or references an ACL-style permission set; partial overlap with the ACL data structure itself
A compiled OPA Policy (IR plan) configures the authorization decisions performed at evaluation time, matching the D3FEND notion of an access-control configuration artefact
Rego policies are commonly used to enforce content-policy decisions (e.g. admission control payloads, HTTP request bodies) although Policy is more general
Rego policies are evaluated via a control-flow-style IR (blocks, statements, breaks) and govern downstream control flow of host applications
A bundle Manifest is a versioned container that packages one or more access-control configurations (compiled Rego policies, data, wasm resolvers)
A capability-declared built-in describes an administrative primitive available to policies (regex, http.send, crypto), partially overlapping access-policy administration
A built-in's declared type signature documents an administrative primitive surface; related to access-policy administration metadata
OPA Capabilities describe what the local authorization engine supports; relates to D3FEND LocalAuthorizationService when OPA is embedded in-process
A WasmResolver dispatches a path to a wasm-embedded entrypoint; commonly used to expose policy decisions as a local authorization service
